sections.paragraph:
πολυτελής, ές (τέλος; Hdt. et al.; ins, pap, LXX; En; TestSol 5:1 D; TestJob; TestJud 26:3; JosAs) pert. to being of great value or worth, ordinarily of relatively high degree on a monetary scale, (very) expensive, costly (so Thu.+; ins, pap, LXX, Philo; Jos., C. Ap. 2, 191) of ointment Mk 14:3. Of clothing (X., An. 1, 5, 8; Diod S 4, 53, 3; 17, 35, 2; Polyaenus 6, 1, 4; Philo, Sacr. Abel. 21; Jos., Bell. 1, 605) 1 Ti 2:9. Of stones (Diod S 1, 33, 3; 2, 16, 4; OGI 90, 34; 132, 8 [s. note 7]; SEG VIII 467, 16 [217 b.c.]; PGM 5, 239. So mostly LXX; En 18:6; EpArist 60 al.) λίθος π. B 6:2 (Is 28:16); pl. MPol 18:2. παρατάξεις π. costly establishments (s. παράταξις 2) Hs 1:1.—Metaph., of inward adornment ἐνώπιον τοῦ θεοῦ πολυτελές (i.e. God appraises it at high value) 1 Pt 3:4.—DELG s.v. τέλος. M-M. Spicq.

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

En En = I Enoch—List 2

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

TestJob TestJob = Testament of Job—List 2

TestJud TestJud = Testament of Judah, s. Test12Patr—List 2

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

SEG SEG = Supplementum Epigraphicum Graecum—List 3

PGM PGM = Papyri Graecae Magicae—List 4

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

  1. pl. = plural

MPol MPol = Martyrdom of Polycarp; after II a.d.—List 1

Hs Hs = Similitudes

i.e.

i.e. = id est (that is)

I.-E. Indo-European

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Spicq Spicq = CS., Lexique théologique du Nouveau Testament—Lists 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 850
